channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: House passes bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed a bill restricting district court judges from issuing nationwide injunctions in a move that would vastly diminish the ability of courts to block President Trump’s policies. Dubbed the No Rogue Rulings Act, the legislation from Rep. Darrell Issa (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the roof collapse at the Jet Set club in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The roof of an iconic nightclub in the Dominican capital collapsed during a merengue concert, killing at least 184 people and injuring over 200 more.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bahamas officials reveal American man's cause of death after body found on beach
item.description: Bahamas police have revealed the cause of death for an American man who briefly disappeared during a vacation with his parents and was found dead the next morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Court of Appeals rejects petition to dismiss indictment of ex-congressional candidate accused of murder
item.description: The Nevada Court of Appeals Wednesday rejected a petition to dismiss the indictment against a former Republican congressional candidate and ex-wrestler charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State leaders tell Trump cabinet members that Utah should run its own federal programs
item.description: Utah's Speaker of the House Mike Schultz is among the top state leaders pushing for Utah to have control over federal programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4

item.title: Three Americans transferred from Congo to US, one arrested in Utah, charged with coup to overthrow government
item.description: Three Americans sentenced to life in prison in Congo were transferred to the U.S., and another was arrested in Utah in connection with an armed coup to overthrow DRC government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shark attack survivor presses her state for an alert system to keep people safe in the water
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama teen who narrowly survived a fierce shark attack last year said she hopes a proposed alert system before state lawmakers can help keep others safe in the water.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as a teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice after recent health complications.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sam's Club plans to open 15 new locations a year, CEO says
item.description: Sam's Club CEO Chris Nicholas says the company plans to open 15 new locations a year "for the foreseeable future.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A California soccer coach charged in a 13-year-old's death is accused of assaulting other teens
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Southern California youth soccer coach charged with killing a 13-year-old boy last month has also been accused of assaulting at least two other teens in recent years, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kansas 9-year-old caught driving parents' truck to school after missing bus: police
item.description: Police in Kansas said a young boy was apprehended after he reportedly took his parents truck on a three-mile drive to school after he missed the bus ride.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs worry US shoppers who buy groceries at Asian supermarkets
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Loyal customers of Asian supermarkets and other grocery stores that specialize in selling imported food heaved a collective sigh of dismay when President Donald Trump announced extra-high U.S. tariffs on goods from dozens of countries.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and KARENA PHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: The victims, Jeik Aniluz Lusi, 18, and Elvis Borques Castillo, 16, both from the Dominican Republic, were identified after “extensive DNA testing.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Four Americans accused of Congo coup attempt now facing federal charges in Salt Lake City
item.description: Four Americans accused of participating in a failed coup attempt in the Democratic Republic of Congo will now go to trial in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Didn't get a Trader Joe's mini pastel tote? You can buy one for over $1,000 on eBay
item.description: And as they did last year, customers were quick to snag the bags. If you weren't among them, you're in luck — but only if you're willing to spend a pretty penny.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kidnapping and conspiracy charges are dropped against college students in ‘Catch a Predator’ fad
item.description: A judge has dismissed conspiracy and kidnapping charges against five Massachusetts college students who were accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on social media.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rich on Tech Radio Recap: Nintendo News, Mesh Wi-Fi & Online Privacy
item.description: You see my segments on TV, but did you know I also host a national radio show called Rich On Tech? Each week, I share helpful tech news, tips, and answer your questions on radio stations across the country — from KFI AM 640 in Los Angeles to 710 WOR in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump, China escalate trade war with new moves and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: About $1.8 billion in federal money for Cornell and Northwestern is frozen, the White House says
item.description: Cornell University and Northwestern University say they haven't received any notice from the U.S. government about paused federal funding even though the White House says more than $1 billion for Cornell and about $790 million for Northwestern have been frozen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Newly released Delphi exhibits include Richard Allen's confession tapes, police interviews
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. – For the first time, the public is hearing convicted Delphi murderer Richard Allen in his own words, including confessions made to his wife while he was held in prison. Trial exhibits showing Allen’s interviews with police on Oct. 13, 2022, and Oct. 26, 2022, have been released.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Youth soccer coach charged in the death of 13-year-old found on Southern California road
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A youth soccer coach has been charged in the death of a 13-year-old whose body was found on the side of a Southern California road.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly distributing fentanyl, meth near Jordan River Trail
item.description: A man was arrested on Monday for allegedly distributing drugs — including suspected fentanyl and methamphetamine — near the Jordan River Trail.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Italy fines Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies $22M for ripping off tourists
item.description: Italy is levying hefty fines against Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies for forcing tourists to pay more to visit the famed historic site.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Chan, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Repeat human smuggler sentenced in South Texas
item.description: A South Texas man who was arrested four times in the past year on human smuggling charges has been sentenced to prison, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9
